title: Crisp Smoky Roasted Peruvian Chicken Pollo A La Brasa
description: Tender and juicy chicken roasted to perfection in a flavorful blend of spices and sunflower oil. A classic Peruvian dish that's both easy to make and impressive to serve.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400idegrees Fahrenheit (200idegrees Celsius).
Rinse the chicken and pat it dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together the achiote paste, garlic, ginger, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the chicken, making sure to get some under the skin as well.
Place the chicken in a bamboo steamer set over a large skillet or saucepan of simmering water, making sure the chicken is not touching the water.
Cover the steamer with a lid and steam the chicken for 30 minutes.
While the chicken is steaming, heat the sunflower oil in a small saucepan over medium heat until it reaches 400idegrees Fahrenheit (200idegrees Celsius).
Use a thermometer to monitor the oil temperature.
Once the oil has reached the correct temperature, place the chicken in the oil and fry for 5-7 minutes on each side, or until it's golden brown and crispy.
Remove the chicken from the oil with a slotted spoon and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly and serving it hot with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve with lime wedges on the side.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
